index.description: L2 index of methodologies in the architecture domain. The retriever reads this after picking architecture from L1 domains.xml. Entries list slug + tier + summary; open the leaf meta.json / content/*.xml for full payload.

methodology.summary: ADR template variant designed for client architecture-review-board (ARB) approval \u2014 names the approver up-front, captures regime constraints, redaction policy, and review-date binding.
Tags every ADR with one-way-door vs two-way-door reversibility so review depth and approval gates match the cost of being wrong.

Quarterly audit pass that walks every accepted/proposed ADR through a 4-signal rubric (code reality match, stakeholder presence, reversal triggers fired, external context change) and moves each to accepted/superseded/deprecated/re-open.

Translates client NDA/MSA data-handling clauses into a per-engagement AI-agent guardrail config \u2014 allowed-tools list, MCP allow-list, telemetry/log overrides, embedding-provider restrictions, audit cadence.
GraphQL federation via Apollo Router creates a unified supergraph from independently deployed domain subgraphs with depth/complexity limits and CI-validated composition.
Three pillars at the gateway edge: Prometheus metrics, structured access logs with correlation ID, and W3C-context OpenTelemetry traces.
Selects the gateway pattern (edge / BFF / per-service / no gateway) and the product (Kong / Traefik / Envoy / APIGW / Apollo Router) based on workload, team, and platform constraints.
Multi-level rate limiting, circuit breakers, exponential-backoff retries with idempotency, and tiered timeouts at the gateway layer.
Layered gateway security: TLS termination at the edge, authentication (API keys, JWT, OAuth 2), authorization, secure headers, and WAF integration.
Weekly arch-health report: hot-spots, debt deltas, risk register movement, one decision-needed item.
Dependencies point inward through concentric circles: Domain (Entities) at the center, then Application (Use Cases), then Interface Adapters, then Frameworks and Drivers at the outside.
Tackles complexity in the heart of software by building a shared model between developers and domain experts using bounded contexts, aggregates, and ubiquitous language.
Application core is isolated from the outside world through ports (interfaces) and adapters (implementations); both inbound and outbound concerns are pluggable.
All coupling points toward the center: Domain Model -> Domain Services -> Application Services -> Infrastructure / UI / Tests at the outermost layer.
Produces a 6-month mentoring curriculum for staff engineers transitioning into architect roles.
Tier-appropriate PR review checklist for the architectural surface; also serves as the solo dev's self-review checklist for AI-generated code.
Captures one architecturally significant decision per ADR with Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives. Lock format; CI-enforce; treat as first-class artefacts.
Produces an architecture proposal: problem, constraints, options, decision, consequences, rollback.
Playbook step to facilitate the weekly architecture review: pre-read, deliberation rules, decision capture, follow-up.
Nine GoF behavioral patterns (Strategy, Observer, Command, State, Chain of Responsibility, Template Method, Mediator, Iterator, Visitor) for controlling object communication at runtime.
Hierarchical diagrams at four levels: System Context, Containers, Components, Code. Locked toolchain (Structurizr / Mermaid / PlantUML) and per-level audience.
Design multi-layer caching (browser → CDN → API gateway → application → database) using the correct pattern per data type: cache-aside, read-through, write-through, write-behind, or write-around.

Well-Architected-Framework-bound cloud-native design pass \u2014 six pillars (operational excellence, security, reliability, performance, cost, sustainability), provider selection, landing zone, VPC, zero-trust, FinOps, DR \u2014 emitting an ADR + IaC scaffold before any cloud resource is created.

Kubernetes pod / Deployment / autoscaling / security manifest patterns \u2014 declarative model, probes, resource requests, PSS, network policies \u2014 emitted as a pre-merge manifest checklist that catches OOM, CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, and unready-pod traffic before production.
Six GoF creational patterns (Factory Method, Abstract Factory, Builder, Prototype, Singleton, Object Pool) and DI for object creation that increases flexibility and reuse.
Build any persistent schema in three sequential passes: conceptual (entities/relationships) → logical (attributes, keys, 3NF, tech-agnostic) → physical (tables, types, indexes, engine-specific).

Pipeline design pass \u2014 medallion bronze/silver/gold tiers, idempotent tasks, DLQs, orchestrator choice (Airflow / Dagster), schema-evolution gates, data-quality validation \u2014 emitting a pipeline spec + orchestrator DAG skeleton.
Framework for choosing among 100+ databases by access pattern, consistency need, scale envelope, and operational fit; output is a documented selection ADR.
Choose between Monolith, Modular Monolith, and Microservices based on team size, DevOps maturity, and deployment-frequency requirements.
Decide whether to build custom software or buy/adopt a commercial solution by scoring strategic differentiation, TCO, time-to-value, and vendor lock-in.
Routes a workload to AWS, Azure, GCP, or none-of-the-above by walking existing-investment, workload-type, and budget-sensitivity branches; emits a provider+runner-up ADR.
Six-phase architecture decision process (problem, options, trade-off, validation, ADR, implementation) that emits a complete decision report with weighted matrix + ADR + rollback plan.
Picks a backend language + framework + datastore by walking team-expertise, performance-need, ecosystem-fit, and hiring-supply branches; emits a stack ADR with rejected alternatives.

Pattern-selection methodology for distributed systems \u2014 Saga (choreography vs orchestration), CQRS, Event Sourcing, Outbox, 2PC/Saga/TCC tradeoffs, Circuit Breaker, Bulkhead, Retry-with-jitter, Idempotency, Leader Election, Rate Limiting \u2014 emitting a per-decision ADR that pins the chosen pattern + its rejected alternatives.

Event-driven architecture pattern selection \u2014 async broker (Kafka / RabbitMQ / SQS), idempotent consumers, past-tense event naming, saga choreography vs orchestration, replay capability \u2014 emitting an ADR + per-event contract that pins the chosen pattern.

Microservice decomposition decision pass \u2014 DDD subdomains, volatility-based extraction, Strangler-Fig migration, database-per-service, communication patterns, anti-patterns \u2014 emitting an ADR per extraction and a service-map that pins ownership + contracts.
Single-deployable architecture with strict module boundaries: one bounded context per module, schema-per-module, public-API-only calls, import-linter enforced in CI.
Single-deployable architecture (Monolith First): vertical-slice or layered internal layout, ACID transactions, single deploy pipeline; ships when team ≤10 and boundaries unclear.

Decision framework for SaaS multi-tenancy isolation \u2014 shared-everything / schema-per-tenant / DB-per-tenant / hybrid \u2014 with quantified blast-radius math, cost economics, compliance scoping, and migration paths between models.

Three-pillar observability design pass \u2014 metrics, logs, traces with SLI/SLO/error-budget instrumentation, OpenTelemetry collector pipelines, sampling strategy, multi-burn-rate alerting, tiered retention \u2014 emitting a per-service observability spec + collector config.

Decision-time protocol that tags every architecture decision as one-way-door or two-way-door at the ADR header, routes one-way doors through a heavier review path (48h cooldown + devil's-advocate reviewer + rollback estimate), and reviews misclassifications quarterly for calibration.
Routes a design problem to a GoF (creational / structural / behavioural) or cloud-native pattern (CQRS, Saga, Circuit Breaker) using problem-signal triggers; emits a pattern-selection ADR.
Defines numeric SLO targets, caching layers, database optimisation tactics, and load-test gates for a service; ships a performance spec + k6 baseline + scaling triggers.
Captures non-functional requirements (NFRs) as ISO-25010 quality scenarios with stimulus-response measurements, prioritised by ATAM utility tree; emits NFR spec + SLI/SLO doc.

Systematic QAA pass binding NFRs to structural decisions \u2014 ISO/IEC 25010:2023 characteristics, 6-part quality-attribute scenarios, utility tree with (Importance, Difficulty) scoring, ATAM-style tradeoff analysis \u2014 every (H,H) scenario gates an executable fitness function in CI.
Service \u00d7 runbook \u00d7 owner \u00d7 alert \u00d7 DR-tier matrix used for the weekly 30-minute pre-incident readiness review; output is a committed, signed-off readiness snapshot.

Reliability design pass binding availability targets to structural decisions \u2014 SLI/SLO/error-budget definitions, fault-tolerance pattern selection (circuit breaker / retry / bulkhead / timeout), graceful degradation tiers, health checks, chaos plan, DR strategy with RPO/RTO and 3-2-1 backups \u2014 emitting a per-service reliability spec.
Pre-authored bundle of five canonical security ADR stubs (auth, encryption, secrets, isolation, residency) that turns a security-by-design audit from blank-page work into a fill-in-choices exercise.
Zero-Trust, defence-in-depth architecture spec: identity + authZ matrix, secrets handling, OWASP ASVS L2 controls, threat-model, and incident-response gates. Output: security spec + STRIDE diagram.
STRIDE-lite per-feature threat-model checklist that runs during a normal design review and produces a 1-page feature-stm.md attached to the design doc.
Routes a workload to the right serverless composition (API Gateway+Lambda, EventBridge, Step Functions, SQS fan-out) and emits a pattern ADR with cost + cold-start trade-offs.
Cold-start mitigation spec for FaaS: runtime + memory + bundle-size + provisioned concurrency / SnapStart choice, with measured before/after p99 numbers.
Cost-reduction report for a serverless workload: memory right-sizing, batching, downstream call reduction, idle-PC removal; ships before/after spend + break-even RPS vs containers.
Foundational checklist for adopting serverless: execution model, statelessness, event sources, observability, vendor lock-in awareness, and IaC discipline. Output: yes/no checklist with rationale.
IaC blueprint for a serverless stack: SAM / CDK / Serverless Framework / Pulumi / Terraform choice, parameterised template with env-aware stages, deployment pipeline, drift detection.
Selects a GoF structural pattern (Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade, Proxy, Flyweight) from concrete symptom signals; emits ADR with cost + alternative + ban on stacking >2 decorators.
Five-phase process (Understand → Scope → Design → Validate → Document) turning a product brief into a buildable architecture package: requirements, NFR back-of-envelope, C4 L1+L2, and ADRs.
Decides Build / Buy / Hybrid by scoring strategic differentiation, 3-year TCO, time-to-value, vendor lock-in, and integration cost. Emits ADR with quantified rationale.
Builds a stakeholder-weighted decision matrix: criteria + weights (sum = 100%), evidence-backed scores per option, conflict identification, and computed weighted totals.
Routes the decision to the right method (Weighted Matrix, ATAM, Cost of Delay, Wardley, Real Options) based on number of options, stakeholder breadth, reversibility, and uncertainty.
ATAM-style trade-off analysis between competing quality attributes (perf, security, cost, maintainability, availability): scenario-pair conflicts, sensitivity points, and resolution rules.
Generates a stakeholder-tailored trade-off briefing that preserves the key risk for every audience (exec, PM, engineer, ops) and writes the ADR Consequences section.
Classifies a piece of technical debt on the Fowler quadrant, sizes the 15-20% debt budget, and emits a debt item with explicit repayment trigger.
Picks one of seven canonical architecture workflows (system-design, review, ADR, tech-eval, ATAM/CBAM, Strangler migration, design-doc review) and emits its named step sequence + role assignments.
